---
title: 'ISA 401 Final Project: Scraped Data'
author: "Mia Weber, Victoria Rhodes, & Helen Kreitzer"
date: "2026-05-04"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

# Scraped Data: Campus Reel Marketing Salaries

```{r}
# define target schools
target_schools <- c(
  "Miami University",
  "Ohio State University",
  "University of Cincinnati",
  "Ohio University",
  "University of Toledo",
  "Bowling Green State University",
  "Cleveland State University",
  "Wright State University",
  "Kent State University",
  "University of Akron",
  "Youngstown State University"
)
```


```{r campusreel_scraping}

# step 0: define URLs for all pages of Ohio marketing rankings
# CampusReel paginates results, so we include all pages to capture all schools
campusreel_urls <- c(
  "https://www.campusreel.org/best-colleges/major/marketing/top-colleges-for-marketing/ohio?filter-order=earnings+DESC&page=1",
  "https://www.campusreel.org/best-colleges/major/marketing/top-colleges-for-marketing/ohio?filter-order=earnings+DESC&page=2",
  "https://www.campusreel.org/best-colleges/major/marketing/top-colleges-for-marketing/ohio?filter-order=earnings+DESC&page=3",
  "https://www.campusreel.org/best-colleges/major/marketing/top-colleges-for-marketing/ohio?filter-order=earnings+DESC&page=4"
)


# step 1: load required libraries
library(rvest)
library(stringr)
library(dplyr)


# step 2: scrape and combine text from all pages
# we loop through each URL and extract all visible text
all_page_text <- ""

for(url in campusreel_urls){
  page <- read_html(url)
  all_page_text <- paste(all_page_text, html_text2(page))
  
  # pause between requests to avoid overwhelming the server
  Sys.sleep(2)
}


# step 3: define a function to extract salary for a given school
# this function searches the combined text for the school name
# and captures the "On average, graduates earn $..." phrase
get_salary_for_school <- function(school_name){
  
  pattern <- paste0(
    school_name,
    "(.|\\n)*?On average, graduates earn \\$[0-9,]+"
  )
  
  block <- str_extract(
    all_page_text,
    regex(pattern, ignore_case = TRUE)
  )
  
  salary <- str_extract(block, "\\$[0-9,]+$")
  
  return(salary)
}


# step 4: apply the function to all target schools
# this creates a structured dataset of school and salary
campusreel_salary_data <- data.frame(
  school = target_schools,
  average_salary = sapply(target_schools, get_salary_for_school),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)


# step 5: clean salary values
# remove $ and commas and convert to numeric for analysis
campusreel_salary_data <- campusreel_salary_data |>
  mutate(
    average_salary_num = as.numeric(gsub("[\\$,]", "", average_salary))
  )


# step 6: reset row names for clean formatting
rownames(campusreel_salary_data) <- NULL


# final output
campusreel_salary_data
```

```{r}
# write CSV file (no row numbers)
write.csv(
  campusreel_salary_data,
  "scraped_campusreel_data.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)
```
